ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: tɔ beʋi ye si, “Wɔ wonu ɔniliɛ gi yɛ su Yohanes apani aplɛ si atrába, loo kudi kudeonu ɔki ɔnɔ dzedze na a?”
translated: Then they said, “Who is this John the Baptist that we should follow his teachings, or is he just a man like us?”

ref: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
text: Lɛ gi niklɔ banɛ ma bɛmɔ Yesu i gyesi i, bɛ kpɛ ole tsisi kusio klɔ petee. Lɛ banima bemani banama gi bɛzɛ nya ɛ dɔni imaɛ nɛ klɔ gi iklani ba mɛ mani Yesu i.
translated: They took branches of palm trees and went out to meet Jesus, shouting, "Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord—the King of Israel!"

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: Lɛ loso e, zi wɔ liʋɛ nɛ tɔ sɛ. Mɛ mapɛ si miki ɔxwɛwa gɔgɔɛ nite gi maki wɔ ɔ.
translated: The child is crying because he is hungry. I have not yet found any food to give him.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Gi bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, belulu pɔ̀ Yesu asi ba ni le onu klɔ si, “Mlamɔ ɔna! Mɛ iliɛ ya! Mlakíse lifu u!”
translated: When he saw him, the crippled man fell at Jesus’ feet and cried out, “Have mercy on me! I am lame! Help me get up!”

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Le kede gi Yesu asɛ ní klɔ ɛ, benyime kuŋupo tuʋa bezeni yɛ kude e, gi bɛzɛɛkpɛ ozi lo si, “Di blɔ asigaga, Dawid Yebi ye!”
translated: When Jesus entered Jerusalem, the crowds spread their clothes on the road, and others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road, shouting, “Hosanna, blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, the Son of David!”

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Ɔniliɛ gi aapɛ si yibu yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, aalila lɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi aalila yɛ ligbã nɛ kɔkpɛ mɛ loso e, aabu le. Liʋɛtsatsa ní Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi kuzɛɛzɛ loso
translated: The world is full of darkness, and the darkness is ignorance. But the world of ignorance is being pushed away by the light. Jesus is the true light that is coming into the world.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Yesu asi ba si, “Kɔlɔ ɛ, mlakɔ la amani mɛ ní ya.”
translated: Jesus said to them, “Come, follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.”

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Agi Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzɛɛ sɛ ní Yerixo e linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ lize ni Yesu kude.
translated: When Jesus was coming from Jericho he saw a blind man sitting by the roadside.

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: lɛ ba bimbi ʋe bidra ɛ. Yesu akpɛ ɔsã gagla aki ba si, “Mlakí pani ní lɛ su i, si ɔnɔnɔ!”
translated: They were very surprised. Jesus looked at them and said, “How hard it is for those who trust in riches to enter the kingdom of God!”

ref: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
text: Yesu ebu abee si, “Abla ɛ kitɛ mlegyo edi ni liboe lɛ ya su u? Onyime ɔtɔ eze gi bebinyime tiaʋa beze ni yɛ awla a. Adzɛ otsitsi ye klɔ lɛ asi yɛ si, ‘Mobi ye, trɛ tɔ zɛɛwa axwɛ na ní wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ omono.’
translated: Jesus said to them, “Do you not yet understand? When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you pick up?”

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Pɔ̀ eboa la gi izeedo ega onugu lo me e, edɔ yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ, lɛ la enu kidiliɛ gi kizelulu ɔnɔ ɔ.
translated: When the child went to the river to fetch water, he saw that the water had dried up, and he returned home with an empty pot.

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: Lɛ bebu isu ya onu si, ‘Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ki blɔ lixwɛxwɛ ɛ.’ Lɛ asi ba si, ‘Lipɛ, nite kɔ mlɔ fɛ mlazɛwa axwɛ na ni mɛ wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ mɛ ɛ.’
translated: The goat said to the dog, ‘Your mouth is very small.’ The dog replied, ‘Yes, but when I bite I don’t let go until blood is drawn.’

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: “Agi ɔlagɔ lɔ inyɔ tiglo isã ɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ, onetee asi yɛ axwɛ na aʋa odidi ye si, ‘Xwa baxwɛwa ba tɔ tsa ba aʋɛ na kí ba. Kpese ní baxwɛwa gɔgɔ ba aʋa zɛkɔna ni baxwɛwa tɔpu ba aʋa a.
translated: When the man got up and went to the farm, he saw that the maize was ripe, so he went home and said, ‘Let us go and harvest the maize. Tomorrow we will finish harvesting the maize and then we will bring it home.’

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Pɔ̀ gi onyimee asɛ ɛ, azeekpese liboe le kufuflu ní ɔkɔ kakaa a. Onyimee yɛ onugu haha lɔ ɛki si Yesu ɔ́zɛɛtani kudo ní ɔha kusi ní ɔma tɔtɔ mɛ ɛ. Kɔ ɛ, ebu isu ya lidze dzaa ní banima su, pɔ̀ banima bɛzɛɛba yɛ klɔ dɔni ɔkɔ kakaa a.
translated: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so they could anoint Jesus’ body. Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they went to the tomb.

ref: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
text: Aloo, mlákla ní Mose Ɔsã nɔ mɛ si ní Lizemɔniwe kakaa aʋa ɛ, kusumu yo baka ba bizɛɛta Mose ɔsã nɔ ese ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ baa pɔ̀ baʋíŋa lipɔ na a?
translated: So, the people said to Moses, “If you are not the one who brought us out from Lizemɔniwe, why do you want to kill us, Moses? Is it because we said to Lisɔligba that we do not want you to rule over us?”

ref: Jesus told them another story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a farmer plants a mustard seed in a field.
text: Yesu ebu abee dzedze aki ba si, “Aya Kaʋaŋaa kalɛ nite e: Onyime ɔtɔ akɔ lisebi sisami nɛ a kpɛ ní yɛ kesea mɛ
translated: Jesus said to them, “Have you never read this: The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
text: Banɔ glegle biaba ní mɛ linyi ne me si, ba benu Kristo ye gi biameni banɔ glegle e.
translated: All the good things that I did not do, I did them for Christ because of the good things he did for me.

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Dɔnɔ abla kɔtsinɔ ɛ, mɛsi mlɔ si, mlatrámɔ mɛ tɔɔ kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ gi mlaapani si, ‘Aya ɔkrira ɔniliɛ gi atrába ní Ɔkaɛ linyi ne me e.’ ”
translated: When the woman arrived, she met the children, and the children asked her, “Where is the man who came to look for your husband?”

ref: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
text: Likpese ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛɛ Yesaya aŋwlimi nɛ kinɛ si: “Aya apani si, ‘Maakpɛ mɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ní wɔ ɔtɔ nɔ tɔ aadra kudeo ki wɔ.’
translated: The elders of the town said to Aya, "Isaiah has written to you, saying: 'Aya, get up, take your child and go to the river to fetch water.'"

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: Ba liboe le linu si, ‘Benyime ba ya gi bɛba kudegba ɛ, ɔnyɔsasa tole ko bɛwa axwɛ na a; lɛ blɔ kɔ ɔ kwize kuweo me e, wa axwɛ na liwe zizi nɛ petee. Pɔ̀ liʋɛ lide tile ko wɔtsa aki blɔ petee!”
translated: When morning came, the children said, “Let’s go to the riverside; the sun is already up and it is bright; we will bathe in the river, and after that we will all eat. Then we will play until the sun sets and we all return home!”

ref: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
text: Pɔ̀ ɔdzɛnyee ebu le onu si mɛɛsi mlɔ si, méte mlo.”
translated: When the child grows up and becomes an adult, I will also grow up.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Maria Magdaladzee ni Maria ʋlaɛ, bezè niklɔ gi bebu aŋu na mɛ kpɛ ɔdrɔ lɔ ɛ. Bedu ɔnɔ si oze pɛ ɔdrɔ lɔ
translated: Mary Magdalene is the woman who was delivered from seven demons.

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Lɛ Kanandze ɔtɔ gi ádɔ ní kusio klɔ ɛ, aba Yesu klɔ lɛ akpɛ ozi si, “Ɔkaɛ Dawid yɛ lipo me obi ye! Di me asigaga! Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔtɔ ɔkpasi mobidzɛ mɛ, lɛ ɛkpɛ yɛ kunugu yo o.”
translated: When Kanandze had finished saying these words, Jesus said to him, “You are the son of David, have mercy on me! My ears are blocked! A blind man like you should not be shouting at me like this.”

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Niklɔ Yesu akpɛ onyimee kude ni ɔsã gagla si,
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd that had gathered around him,

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Nite si mlaate ba gani ba kiboebitɛɛ mɛ ɛ. Litsixwawe le su bidɔ basɛbasɛ
translated: They say that the people of Kiboebitɛɛ are many. The litsixwawe are more than all of them.
